title: The Measurement Gap in AI and Doctrinal Reasoning in EU Law
brief: The integration of large language models (LLMs) into legal practice raises significant questions regarding their ability to perform doctrinal legal reasoning.

title: The Complex Reality of Stablecoin Transactions
brief: The prevalent assumption that stablecoin transactions represent straightforward payments is increasingly challenged by emerging analyses.

title: When AI Systems Make Contracting Errors: Analyzing Legal Implications
brief: The increasing reliance on AI for contract formation raises significant legal questions, particularly when mistakes occur.

title: When Algorithms Encounter Contractual Mistakes: Insights from Frattone
brief: As contracting becomes increasingly automated, the potential for algorithmic errors raises significant legal questions.

title: Algorithmic Errors and the Legal Doctrine of Mistake
brief: Navigating Automated Contractual Failures Algorithmic execution introduces unique tensions when automated systems produce contracts based on incorrect assumptions or erroneous pricing.

title: Legal Frameworks for Automated Contracting
brief: Evaluating Autonomy in Algorithmic Agreements The rise of automated contracting presents a fundamental challenge to traditional legal concepts of agency and intent.
